frameworks.name: ring attractor network
frameworks.one_line: head-direction attractor retuned so activity amplitude tracks posterior certainty

frameworks.name: circular Kalman filter
frameworks.one_line: normative benchmark for angular inference under uncertainty

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: memory should weigh evidence by uncertainty when combining with priors

core_question: How can a ring attractor network represent and update uncertainty during angular working memory?
key_assumptions: working memory is held in attractor networks
uncertainty must be tracked to weigh memory against new evidence
head direction is encoded by a ring attractor
recurrent connectivity can be retuned without exact precision
manipulations: recurrent connectivity tuning of the ring attractor
quality and consistency of incoming evidence
key_findings: retuned ring attractor matches circular Kalman filter benchmark
network activity amplitude grows with confirmatory evidence and shrinks with poor or conflicting evidence
Bayesian ring attractor is consistently more accurate than a conventional ring attractor
near-optimal performance is preserved under connectome-derived biological constraints
interpretations: amplitude of attractor activity can serve as a neural code for posterior certainty
biologically plausible recurrent circuits can implement near-optimal Bayesian inference
